title: Inventory Local Doom Reference Files
summary: Current-state inventory of source-only metadata for the Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 / vanilla DOOM 1.9 reference declaration surface across four committed src/ TypeScript modules and one committed reference/manifests/ JSON file that together declare the local doom/ reference bundle's identity, license boundary, sandbox copy policy, and reference-run manifest without embedding the proprietary bundle bytes themselves: src/reference/policy.ts (103 lines, ASSET_BOUNDARIES classification of 8 reference bundle files into commercial-shareware/gpl/mixed/utility license categories with forbidden/permitted-with-notice redistribution policies, plus the REFERENCE_BUNDLE_PATH and CODEX_WORKSPACE_PATH absolute path constants resolved relative to the package root via node:path.resolve), src/reference/target.ts (49 lines, the PRIMARY_TARGET frozen anchor pinning engine='Chocolate Doom', engineVersion='2.2.1', emulatedVersion='1.9', gameMode='shareware', executableHash='5CA97717FD79F833B248AE5985FFFADBEE39533F5E0529BC9D7B439E08B8F1D2', dosExecutableHash='9D3216605417888D5A699FA3794E46638EB4CB8634501AE748D74F9C2CFAE37B', wadHash='1D7D43BE501E67D927E415E0B8F3E29C3BF33075E859721816F652A526CAC771', wadFilename='DOOM1.WAD', wadLumpCount=1264, ticRateHz=35), src/oracles/referenceSandbox.ts (139 lines, SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES of 4 entries with role+expectedHash+expectedSize+mutableDuringRun records, SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES of 4 entries with explicit reasons, REFERENCE_SANDBOX_POLICY pinning sandboxPrefix='sandbox-', sandboxParent='.sandboxes', verifyHashesAfterCopy=true, cleanupAfterRun=true, copyConfigsAsMutable=true), src/oracles/referenceRunManifest.ts (205 lines, REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST with INIT_SEQUENCE_LENGTH=15, ticRateHz=35, emulatedVersion='1.9', screen 320x200 internal / 640x480 display / 32bpp, audio 44100 Hz / 8 channels / OPL port 0x388, startup skill=2 episode=1 map=1 deathmatch=0, vanillaCompatibility demoLimit/keyboardMapping/savegameLimit all true, runModes demo-playback + title-loop), and reference/manifests/file-hashes.json (56 lines, the SHA-256 + sizeBytes + role manifest of all 8 files in the universal-doom reference bundle: DOOM.EXE, DOOM1.WAD, DOOMD.EXE, DOOMDUPX.EXE, DOOMWUPX.exe, chocolate-doom.cfg, default.cfg, smash.py). The four sandbox-required filenames (DOOM.EXE, DOOM1.WAD, chocolate-doom.cfg, default.cfg) are the exact subset the prior 01-018 tools/reference/ capture surface inventory pinned as REQUIRED_FILE_COUNT=4 (mutable: chocolate-doom.cfg + default.cfg; immutable: DOOM.EXE + DOOM1.WAD), and the four sandbox-excluded filenames (DOOMD.EXE, DOOMDUPX.EXE, DOOMWUPX.exe, smash.py) match the four ASSET_BOUNDARIES entries excluded by the host-platform / utility-tool justifications recorded in src/oracles/referenceSandbox.ts. Companion tests at test/reference/license-boundary.test.ts (136 lines, exercises ASSET_BOUNDARIES license category and redistribution policy invariants), test/reference/target.test.ts (60 lines, exercises PRIMARY_TARGET hash and constant invariants), test/reference/hash-manifest.test.ts (76 lines, exercises file-hashes.json shape and PRIMARY_TARGET cross-reference), test/oracles/reference-sandbox-policy.test.ts (226 lines, exercises SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES + SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES + REFERENCE_SANDBOX_POLICY shape with file-hashes.json cross-reference), and test/oracles/reference-run-manifest.test.ts (278 lines, exercises REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST shape with INIT_SEQUENCE_LENGTH consistency check) are inventoried for size_bytes, line_count, and sha256 only. The doom/ reference bundle directory itself is NOT inventoried here because it is read-only outside this inventory's read-only paths; the source-only metadata captured here is what the codebase declares ABOUT the bundle, not the bundle's bytes.
evidence_method: inventory lane local repository audit using readdirSync enumeration of src/reference/ and src/oracles/ and reference/manifests/, statSync for size_bytes, line counting via readFileSync split('\n') with trailing-newline normalization matching the prior 01-018 countLines() helper, sha256sum-equivalent SHA-256 fingerprints over the raw file bytes, regex extraction of `^export (const|function|interface|type|class)` declarations from each TypeScript module, Grep enumeration of importing files to derive src_importer_count and test_importer_count, and JSON-encoded constant capture for the ASSET_BOUNDARIES (8 entries with 4 license categories and 2 redistribution policies), the PRIMARY_TARGET pinned identity (engine, version, emulatedVersion, hashes, wad metadata, ticRateHz), the SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES (4 entries with role + mutability), the SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES (4 entries with reasons), and the REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST invariants (INIT_SEQUENCE_LENGTH=15, runModes count=2, screen/audio/startup/vanillaCompatibility records). Companion test files at test/reference/license-boundary.test.ts, test/reference/target.test.ts, test/reference/hash-manifest.test.ts, test/oracles/reference-sandbox-policy.test.ts, and test/oracles/reference-run-manifest.test.ts are inventoried for size_bytes, line_count, and sha256 only; their internal test-case enumeration is not captured here. The doom/ reference bundle directory is intentionally NOT enumerated because it lies outside this step's read-only paths and outside any plan_vanilla_parity write lock; bundle-content cataloging is the responsibility of the prior 02-001 oracle-lane catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs step. plan_engine/ and plan_fps/ are listed as read-only paths in the step contract but are NOT enumerated here — their reference-related artifacts are prior art under plan_vanilla_parity governance and any source-level constant they reference is already pinned by this inventory's SHA-256 fingerprints over the live src/reference/ and src/oracles/ files.

source_modules.role: Declares the REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST frozen ReferenceRunManifest record describing exactly how to invoke the reference Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 executable for oracle capture, the initial state it produces, and the available run modes. Pins INIT_SEQUENCE_LENGTH=15 as the canonical Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 startup-sequence step count (Z_Init through I_InitStretchTables), the 320x200 internal and 640x480 display screen dimensions with 32bpp output and aspect-ratio-correct=true, the 44100 Hz sample rate with 8 max audio channels and OPL I/O port 0x388, the startup parameters skill=2 episode=1 map=1 deathmatch=0 playerCount=1 totalNodes=1, the vanilla compatibility flags demoLimit/keyboardMapping/savegameLimit all true, ticRateHz=35, emulatedVersion='1.9', and the 2-element runModes array (demo-playback for deterministic frame-by-frame replay, title-loop for full attract-cycle capture). Exposes the RunMode discriminant type, the RunModeConfig/ScreenParameters/AudioParameters/StartupParameters/VanillaCompatibility/InitStep/ReferenceRunManifest interface family, the INIT_SEQUENCE_LENGTH integer constant, and the REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST frozen record consumed by oracle-capture and bootstrap test paths.

Declares the SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES frozen ASCIIbetically-sorted array of 4 SandboxFileEntry records (DOOM.EXE executable + DOOM1.WAD iwad both immutable; chocolate-doom.cfg + default.cfg both config-role mutable) with per-file expectedHash (uppercase hex SHA-256) and expectedSize (bytes), the SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES frozen array of 4 ExcludedFileEntry records (DOOMD.EXE / DOOMDUPX.EXE / DOOMWUPX.exe / smash.py) with explicit host-platform or utility-tool reasons, and the REFERENCE_SANDBOX_POLICY frozen SandboxPolicy object pinning sandboxPrefix='sandbox-', sandboxParent='.sandboxes', sourcePath=REFERENCE_BUNDLE_PATH, workspaceRoot=CODEX_WORKSPACE_PATH, verifyHashesAfterCopy=true, cleanupAfterRun=true, and copyConfigsAsMutable=true. Exposes the SandboxFileRole 'config'|'executable'|'iwad' discriminant type, the SandboxFileEntry / ExcludedFileEntry / SandboxPolicy interfaces, and the three frozen constants consumed by the prior 01-018 tools/reference/isolationProbe.ts capture surface and the test/oracles/reference-sandbox-policy.test.ts companion test.

Declares the ASSET_BOUNDARIES frozen ASCIIbetically-sorted array of 8 AssetBoundary records that classify every file in the doom/ reference bundle into a license category (commercial-shareware | gpl | mixed | utility) and redistribution policy (forbidden | permitted-with-notice) with a short licenseNote: DOOM.EXE (mixed/forbidden polyglot id+GPL binary), DOOM1.WAD (commercial-shareware/forbidden id Software shareware IWAD), DOOMD.EXE (commercial-shareware/forbidden DOS shareware), DOOMDUPX.EXE (commercial-shareware/forbidden UPX-packed DOS), DOOMWUPX.exe (gpl/permitted-with-notice UPX-packed Chocolate Doom Windows), chocolate-doom.cfg (gpl/permitted-with-notice host config), default.cfg (gpl/permitted-with-notice vanilla defaults), smash.py (utility/permitted-with-notice merge tool). Also declares REFERENCE_BUNDLE_PATH (absolute path to doom/) and CODEX_WORKSPACE_PATH (absolute path to package root) resolved relative to import.meta.dir via node:path.resolve. Exposes the LicenseCategory and RedistributionPolicy discriminant types, the AssetBoundary interface, and the ASSET_BOUNDARIES / REFERENCE_BUNDLE_PATH / CODEX_WORKSPACE_PATH constants consumed across src/, test/, and tools/.

Declares the PRIMARY_TARGET frozen ReferenceTarget record pinning the exact identity of the reference engine and IWAD that all oracle captures and parity tests are measured against: engine='Chocolate Doom', engineVersion='2.2.1', emulatedVersion='1.9' (the vanilla DOOM version Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 emulates), gameMode='shareware', executableHash (SHA-256 of merged polyglot DOOM.EXE), dosExecutableHash (SHA-256 of DOS-only DOOMD.EXE), wadHash (SHA-256 of DOOM1.WAD shareware IWAD), wadFilename='DOOM1.WAD', wadLumpCount=1264, ticRateHz=35. Exposes the ReferenceTarget interface and the PRIMARY_TARGET frozen constant consumed across test/reference/, test/oracles/, test/parity/, test/wad/, test/audio/, test/map/, test/launcher/, test/bootstrap/, test/_harness/, src/main.ts, and src/reference/target.ts (self).

manifest_files.role: JSON manifest declaring the SHA-256 fingerprint, sizeBytes, and role for all 8 files in the universal-doom reference bundle: DOOM.EXE (merged-polyglot-executable, 1893888 bytes), DOOM1.WAD (shareware-iwad, 4196020 bytes), DOOMD.EXE (dos-executable, 709753 bytes), DOOMDUPX.EXE (dos-executable-upx, 379638 bytes), DOOMWUPX.exe (windows-executable-upx, 475648 bytes), chocolate-doom.cfg (engine-config, 3838 bytes), default.cfg (default-config, 1565 bytes), smash.py (merge-tool, 3191 bytes). The manifest's algorithm field is 'SHA-256' and its source field documents the original D:\Projects\bun-win32\doom\universal-doom location at generation time. The file is a static metadata document, not a TypeScript module, so it has no exports; it is consumed by JSON-imported tests via Bun's native JSON module support.

asset_boundary_invariants.notes: ASSET_BOUNDARIES classifies all 8 files in the doom/ reference bundle. The four redistribution-forbidden filenames are DOOM.EXE (mixed-license polyglot) plus the three commercial-shareware DOS binaries DOOM1.WAD/DOOMD.EXE/DOOMDUPX.EXE. The four redistribution-permitted filenames are the GPL Chocolate Doom Windows binary DOOMWUPX.exe + the two GPL config files chocolate-doom.cfg + default.cfg + the utility smash.py merge tool. Future policy changes (for example reclassifying a file from commercial-shareware to mixed) require updates to both src/reference/policy.ts and this inventory snapshot. The 'mixed' classification of DOOM.EXE is load-bearing: the polyglot binary contains both id Software shareware DOS code AND GPL Chocolate Doom Windows code, so it inherits the commercial-shareware redistribution restriction even though the GPL portion is independently redistributable.

primary_target_invariants.notes: PRIMARY_TARGET is the single source-of-truth anchor that pins the reference identity all oracle captures and parity tests are measured against. The dosExecutableHash field is intentionally distinct from executableHash because DOOMD.EXE (the DOS-only shareware binary) and DOOM.EXE (the merged polyglot containing both DOS code and Chocolate Doom Windows code) have different bytes and different SHA-256 fingerprints. The wadLumpCount=1264 is the post-IWAD-load lump count (excluding any PWAD overrides) for the shareware DOOM1.WAD; future plan_vanilla_parity Phase 02 oracle steps that load alternate IWADs (registered, ultimate, doom2, plutonia, tnt) will need to extend this anchor with additional pinned hashes rather than mutating the shareware values. The ticRateHz=35 matches REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST.ticRateHz and is the canonical vanilla DOOM 1.9 tic rate; any oracle-capture step that needs a different tic rate must define a new manifest, not override this constant.

sandbox_required_files_invariants.notes: SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES enumerates exactly the 4 files that must be copied into a reference sandbox before the reference Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 process can launch reproducibly. The two configs (chocolate-doom.cfg, default.cfg) are mutable because Chocolate Doom may write settings on exit; the executable + iwad are immutable because they are the binary identity being measured. The expected_hash_length=64 invariant reflects the SHA-256 hex string length and matches the regex pattern test/oracles/reference-sandbox-policy.test.ts enforces. The required filenames are exactly the subset of ASSET_BOUNDARIES that are eligible for sandbox copy after applying the 4-entry SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES filter (which removes DOOMD.EXE/DOOMDUPX.EXE/DOOMWUPX.exe/smash.py).

sandbox_excluded_files_invariants.notes: SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES enumerates exactly the 4 files in the doom/ reference bundle that are NOT copied into the sandbox, each with a justifying reason: DOOMD.EXE and DOOMDUPX.EXE are DOS-only binaries that cannot execute on the Windows host; DOOMWUPX.exe is the UPX-packed alternative when DOOM.EXE is the primary executable; smash.py is the bundle merge utility used to build the polyglot at packaging time, not to run the engine at oracle-capture time. The disjoint partition required_filenames + excluded_filenames covers all 8 ASSET_BOUNDARIES filenames; future plan_vanilla_parity steps that introduce a new bundle file must extend either SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES or SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES (and update both src/reference/policy.ts ASSET_BOUNDARIES and this inventory snapshot).

reference_run_manifest_invariants.notes: REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST captures every observable quantity of a Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 reference run as a single frozen record. INIT_SEQUENCE_LENGTH=15 is the canonical step count of the engine's startup sequence from Z_Init (zone memory) through I_InitStretchTables (lookup-table generation); future Chocolate Doom version bumps would change this count and would require updating both src/oracles/referenceRunManifest.ts and this inventory. The aspect_ratio_correct=true and 640x480 display values are derived from chocolate-doom.cfg; the 320x200 internal values are vanilla DOOM constants. The OPL I/O port 0x388 is the canonical AdLib OPL2 base port that Chocolate Doom and vanilla DOOM both target. The 2 run modes (demo-playback for deterministic frame-by-frame replay; title-loop for full attract-cycle capture) match the prior 01-018 capture pipeline assumptions and the prior 01-014 demo-and-replay inventory's recorded surface.

boundary_status.notes: src/reference/, src/oracles/, and reference/manifests/ are writable workspaces under plan_vanilla_parity governance. The doom/ reference bundle directory is read-only outside this inventory's read-only paths (it is not listed under src/, test/, tools/, plan_engine/, plan_fps/, reference/manifests/, or package.json) and is therefore intentionally NOT enumerated by this inventory; bundle-content cataloging belongs to the prior 02-001 oracle-lane catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs step. The four sandbox-required filenames (DOOM.EXE, DOOM1.WAD, chocolate-doom.cfg, default.cfg) and the four sandbox-excluded filenames (DOOMD.EXE, DOOMDUPX.EXE, DOOMWUPX.exe, smash.py) together form a disjoint partition of the 8 ASSET_BOUNDARIES filenames — the consistency invariant this inventory pins. No proprietary id Software bytes are embedded in any of the inventoried source files or this inventory; every bundle file is referenced by filename + SHA-256 fingerprint only.

implications: src/reference/policy.ts is the single source-of-truth that classifies every file in the doom/ reference bundle by license category (commercial-shareware | gpl | mixed | utility) and redistribution policy (forbidden | permitted-with-notice). Future plan_vanilla_parity steps that add or remove a file from the bundle must update ASSET_BOUNDARIES and propagate the change through SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES / SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES at src/oracles/referenceSandbox.ts (which the prior 01-018 tools/reference/isolationProbe.ts capture surface inventory pinned as REQUIRED_FILE_COUNT=4 / MUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2 / IMMUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2) and through reference/manifests/file-hashes.json (which the test/reference/hash-manifest.test.ts companion test pins as exactly 8 file entries). The 'mixed' classification of DOOM.EXE forces the polyglot binary into the redistribution-forbidden bucket because it inherits the commercial-shareware restriction from its id Software DOS portion even though its Chocolate Doom Windows portion is independently GPL.

src/reference/target.ts pins the PRIMARY_TARGET reference identity (engine='Chocolate Doom', engineVersion='2.2.1', emulatedVersion='1.9', gameMode='shareware', SHA-256 hashes for DOOM.EXE/DOOMD.EXE/DOOM1.WAD, wadFilename='DOOM1.WAD', wadLumpCount=1264, ticRateHz=35) that all 68+ test files cross-reference. The wadLumpCount=1264 is specific to the shareware IWAD; future plan_vanilla_parity Phase 02 oracle-capture steps that target a different IWAD (registered, ultimate, doom2, plutonia, tnt) must define a new pinned target rather than mutating PRIMARY_TARGET. The ticRateHz=35 in PRIMARY_TARGET matches REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST.ticRateHz exactly — these two anchors must stay in lockstep, and any future change requires updating both modules and this inventory.

src/oracles/referenceSandbox.ts SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES and SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES are the load-bearing partition that the prior 01-018 tools/reference/isolationProbe.ts capture surface depends on: the probe enforces REQUIRED_FILE_COUNT=4 / MUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2 / IMMUTABLE_FILE_COUNT=2 by counting filtered SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES entries. Adding a new file (for example a savegame.dsg snapshot or a screenshot.bmp) requires extending SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES with role+expectedHash+expectedSize+mutableDuringRun and propagating the new MUTABLE_FILE_COUNT / IMMUTABLE_FILE_COUNT through tools/reference/isolationProbe.ts AND the prior 01-018 inventory snapshot AND this 01-019 inventory snapshot. Removing a file requires the inverse propagation plus a SANDBOX_EXCLUDED_FILES entry with a justifying reason.

src/oracles/referenceRunManifest.ts INIT_SEQUENCE_LENGTH=15 pins the canonical Chocolate Doom 2.2.1 startup sequence step count (Z_Init through I_InitStretchTables). This is the foundation for the 02-005 capture-doomd-clean-launch-feasibility and 02-006 capture-doom-exe-clean-launch-feasibility oracle steps that capture observed initialization stdout against this manifest. A future Chocolate Doom version bump (for example 3.0.x) would change INIT_SEQUENCE_LENGTH and require defining a new manifest rather than mutating REFERENCE_RUN_MANIFEST. The screen 320x200 internal / 640x480 display / 32bpp values match the prior 01-018 GDI capture pipeline invariants exactly — the framebuffer-capture path and the reference-run manifest cannot drift apart.

reference/manifests/file-hashes.json is the JSON source-of-truth that test/reference/hash-manifest.test.ts cross-references against PRIMARY_TARGET (executableHash, dosExecutableHash, wadHash) and that test/oracles/reference-sandbox-policy.test.ts cross-references against every SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES entry's expectedHash + expectedSize. Any new bundle file added to ASSET_BOUNDARIES must also be added to file-hashes.json and tested against either PRIMARY_TARGET or SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES; this 8-entry consistency invariant is the gate that prevents the four declaration sources (ASSET_BOUNDARIES + PRIMARY_TARGET + SANDBOX_REQUIRED_FILES + file-hashes.json) from drifting apart.

Because this inventory records on-disk SHA-256 fingerprints for every source module, every manifest, and every companion test, any structural rewrite of src/reference/policy.ts, src/reference/target.ts, src/oracles/referenceSandbox.ts, src/oracles/referenceRunManifest.ts, reference/manifests/file-hashes.json, or any of the five companion tests will diverge from the captured snapshot and require a follow-up update to this inventory. The focused inventory test enforces that divergence by recomputing each fingerprint at test time against the live filesystem, so a stale inventory will fail rather than silently mask a declaration rewrite that breaks the reference identity contract. This includes Prettier or Biome reformatting passes that change whitespace — the inventory is intentionally byte-exact.

The doom/ reference bundle directory is intentionally NOT enumerated by this inventory because it lies outside this step's read-only paths. plan_vanilla_parity Phase 02 oracle-lane work owns bundle-content cataloging (02-001 catalog-local-reference-binaries-and-configs, 02-002 verify-local-reference-file-hashes); the inventory lane work captured here owns only the source-level declarations ABOUT the bundle. This split is intentional: the inventory lane proves what the codebase claims, and the oracle lane proves the doom/ directory matches those claims byte-for-byte against expectations.
